entities.name: Fitbit
entities.context: Wearable device used as data source for heart rate, sleep, and workout tracking

entities.name: intraday_heart_rate
entities.context: Database table storing per-minute heart rate readings with BPM, zone, and source

entities.name: sleep_sessions
entities.context: Database table storing normalized sleep log data including duration, efficiency, and main sleep flag

entities.name: sleep_stages
entities.context: Database table storing granular sleep stage segments (e.g. deep, light, REM) per sleep session

entities.name: SQLite
entities.context: Database engine used for local health data storage (sqlite3 module referenced)

entities.name: Heart Rate Zones
entities.context: Classification of heart rate readings into zones, stored in the intraday_heart_rate table

entities.name: Sleep Efficiency
entities.context: Metric tracked per sleep session representing sleep quality as a ratio

entities.name: Main Sleep Detection
entities.context: Boolean flag distinguishing primary sleep from naps in the sleep_sessions schema

entities.name: Health Data Pipeline
entities.context: System architecture for ingesting, normalizing, and storing biometric data from wearables

relationships.context: Fitbit is a source of heart rate data stored in this table
Fitbit sleep logs are normalized into this table
Sleep stages have a foreign key relationship to sleep sessions (sleep_log_id → log_id)
Fitbit serves as the primary wearable data source for the health tracking pipeline
Pipeline stores normalized biometric data in SQLite databases
Efficiency metric is a column in the sleep_sessions table
Zone classification stored alongside each heart rate reading
